context: Tests whether the agent designs the financial audit trail database schema with the correct column types, indexes, and immutability enforcement. The agent is asked to produce the SQL DDL for a new audit trail system for a fintech startup.

checklist.name: BIGSERIAL seq column
checklist.description: The schema includes a BIGSERIAL (or equivalent auto-incrementing bigint) seq column, separate from the UUID primary key, for gap detection

checklist.name: actor_ip INET type
checklist.description: The actor_ip column uses the PostgreSQL INET data type (not VARCHAR or TEXT)

checklist.name: JSONB for state columns
checklist.description: before_state, after_state, and delta columns use the JSONB data type (not JSON or TEXT)

checklist.name: hash and prev_hash columns
checklist.description: Both a hash column and a prev_hash column are present in the schema (VARCHAR(64) or equivalent)

checklist.name: INSERT SELECT only grant
checklist.description: The SQL includes a GRANT statement that gives the application role only INSERT and SELECT privileges — does NOT grant UPDATE or DELETE

checklist.name: No UPDATE/DELETE to app role
checklist.description: There is no GRANT of UPDATE or DELETE on the audit table to the application role (a comment explicitly revoking or noting their absence also satisfies this)

checklist.name: Aggregate lookup index
checklist.description: An index on (aggregate_type, aggregate_id, occurred_at DESC) is created for efficient per-entity lookups

checklist.name: Actor activity index
checklist.description: An index on (actor_id, occurred_at DESC) is created for actor activity timeline queries

checklist.name: Seq-only index
checklist.description: A separate index on the seq column alone is created for chronological scans and compliance exports

checklist.name: Event type index
checklist.description: An index on (event_type, occurred_at DESC) is created for filtering by event type

checklist.name: Separate schema or DB
checklist.description: The DDL places the table in a separate schema (e.g. CREATE TABLE audit.financial_audit_events) or the SQL includes a comment/statement indicating a separate database or schema should be used
